subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Boards & Shared Objects
- **1** warp
- **1** hyperspace gate
- **1** genesis planet
- **1** quick-start guide

#### Player Pieces
- **25** player planets (5 per player)
- **100** plastic ships (20 per player)
- **5** player colony markers

#### Tokens
- **42** cosmic tokens
- **7** grudge tokens
- **1** Prometheus token
- **1** lunar cannon token

#### Alien Content
- **51** alien sheets
- **51** flare cards (1 per alien)
- **34** cosmic combo cards
- **1** alternate Filch flare card

#### Decks
- **55** encounter cards
- **6** reinforcement cards
- **11** artifact cards
- **20** destiny cards
- **20** tech cards

#### Player-Count Reference List (Step 0 Audit)
- **Supported game size** — 3-5 players.
- **Planet setup** — Each player always starts with 5 planets.
- **Ship setup** — Each player always starts with 20 ships total and 4 ships on each home planet.
- **Destiny color cards** — Use 3 destiny cards per chosen player color.
- **Fewer-than-5 rule** — Remove destiny cards of any unchosen colors.
- **Alien selection scaling** — Use the alien sheets indicated by the revealed cosmic combo card for 3-player, 4-player, or 5-player game size.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose color** — Each player takes matching colony marker, 5 planets, and 20 ships.
2. **Build home systems** — Each player places 5 planets in front of them and stacks 4 ships on each.
3. **Center warp** — Place the warp in the center of the table.
4. **Colony trackers** — Place each colony marker at 0 on the colony track.
5. **Shared reach** — Keep hyperspace gate and shared decks/tokens accessible to all players.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Personal loadout** — Each player starts with one full color set: planets, ships, colony marker.
- **Home baseline** — Each home planet starts with 4 ships.
- **Alien assignment** — After cosmic combo reveal, each player gets 1 eligible alien sheet.
- **Starting hand** — Deal 8 cosmic cards to each player.
- **Hand privacy** — Players may look at their own cards but may not show them.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Alien pool** — Use only aliens allowed by the 3-player cosmic combo line.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Alien pool** — Use only aliens allowed by the 4-player cosmic combo line.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Alien pool** — Use only aliens allowed by the 5-player cosmic combo line.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Build destiny deck** — Take 3 destiny cards for each chosen player color.
2. **Add universal cards** — Shuffle those with wild and special destiny cards.
3. **Choose aliens** — Shuffle cosmic combo cards, reveal top card, gather matching alien sheets for this player count, then deal 1 alien sheet to each player.
4. **Build cosmic deck** — Shuffle encounter, reinforcement, and artifact cards together.
5. **Deal cards** — Deal 8 cosmic cards to each player.
6. **Choose first player** — First game: youngest starts; otherwise reveal destiny cards until a chosen color appears, that color starts, then reshuffle destiny deck.

#### Destiny Deck by Player Count
--- 3 Players ---
- **Color destiny cards** — Use 9 color cards (3 cards x 3 chosen colors).
- **Removed color cards** — Remove 6 color cards for the 2 unchosen colors.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Color destiny cards** — Use 12 color cards (3 cards x 4 chosen colors).
- **Removed color cards** — Remove 3 color cards for the 1 unchosen color.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Color destiny cards** — Use all 15 color cards (3 cards x 5 chosen colors).
- **Removed color cards** — Remove 0 color cards.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
1. **Start Turn** — Begin active player's encounter cycle.
2. **Regroup** — Resolve regroup step effects.
3. **Destiny** — Reveal destiny card to determine encounter target/opponent constraints.
4. **Launch** — Offense launches ships through hyperspace gate to target planet.
5. **Alliance** — Players ally with offense or defense as allowed.
6. **Planning** — Main players choose encounter cards (with optional planning effects).
7. **Reveal** — Main players reveal encounter cards simultaneously.
8. **Resolution** — Resolve winner, compensation/deals, and ship/colony outcomes, then continue flow.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Main Encounter Choices
- **Attack vs Attack** — Compare total (ships + attack value + modifiers); higher total wins.
- **Negotiate interactions** — Resolve deal/compensation outcomes per revealed card combination and timing.
- **Card timing** — Apply reinforcement and artifact cards only in their printed legal windows.

#### Alliance Choices
- **Join side** — Eligible players may ally with offense or defense.
- **Ship commitment** — Allies contribute ships, affecting totals and result sharing.
- **Result share** — Allies share rewards/risks based on encounter outcome.

#### Alien-Power Overrides
- **Rule bending** — Alien powers can modify normal permissions and limits.
- **Phase specificity** — Many powers apply only in named phases (e.g., ally timing, planning, resolution).
- **Examples in excerpt** — Some aliens can self-invite to alliances, alter main-player ship count before reveal, or steal used encounter cards.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Asymmetry Core
- **Alien identity** — Every player has a unique alien power that can override core rules.
- **Mindset impact** — Playing to your alien's style is a core strategic lever, not flavor text.

#### Destiny Pressure
- **Target system** — Destiny cards drive who can be targeted each encounter.
- **Table politics** — Destiny outcomes shape alliance bargaining and threat management.

#### Social Economy
- **Negotiation weight** — Diplomacy is as important as card math in most games.
- **Alliance leverage** — Temporary deals can swing a single encounter or the whole game arc.

#### Multi-Deck Tactics
- **Cosmic deck mix** — Encounter, reinforcement, and artifact cards create layered timing decisions.
- **Advanced modules** — Tech, flares, and special tokens expand tactical options when used.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **Scoring model** — There is no VP track and no endgame point conversion.
- **Win trigger** — A player wins by establishing 5 foreign colonies.
- **Foreign colony definition** — Colony must be on another player's planets, not your own home planets.
- **Shared-win rule** — If two or more players establish their fifth foreign colony at the same time, all of them win together.
- **Tiebreaker need** — No additional tiebreaker is used beyond the simultaneous shared-win rule.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Power timing first** — Learn your alien's exact trigger windows before first encounter.
- **Hand preservation** — Keep flexible attack/negotiation options instead of burning best cards in low-impact fights.
- **Early diplomacy** — Build at least one reliable ally channel in the first cycle of encounters.
- **Destiny tracking** — Track active colors and recent flips to estimate likely near-term targeting.
- **Threat posture** — Take colonies steadily without becoming the easiest table-wide target too early.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Math-only play** — Treating the game as pure combat arithmetic ignores negotiation win equity.
- **Ignoring shared wins** — Letting multiple opponents approach 5 colonies can end the game suddenly.
- **Over-launching ships** — Committing too many early ships weakens defense and future launch flexibility.
- **Timing misuse** — Playing artifact/reinforcement cards outside their strongest windows wastes swing potential.
- **Underusing alien power** — Playing generic turns instead of power-shaped turns forfeits your main advantage.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Pace vs exposure** — Push for fast colony count or pace growth to avoid full-table retaliation.
- **Spend vs hold cards** — Cash strong cards for immediate colony pressure or hold for defense/counterplay moments.
- **Alliance choice** — Take short-term ally rewards now or block leader momentum even at personal cost.

metadata.notes: Written from the provided Cosmic Encounter quick-start/rule excerpt. Full timing minutiae and advanced module details are in the complete rulebook; this guide captures the core legal flow and setup from supplied text.
